page: Amazon Web Services r8gn.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 32 dedicated physical cores on the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 architecture running at 2.8 GHz, with no hyperthreading, resulting in a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 8.0 GB per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, lacks local NVMe storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier memory performance, with uncached large-block read bandwidth reaching 414.89 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 186.46 GB/sec. Passmark memory and database operations benchmarks both place in the top tier, with database operations registering 47,350.52. Multi-core performance is also strong, as evidenced by a top-tier Geekbench multi-core score of 21,290.0 and a strong Redis SET throughput of 67.03 million operations per second. However, single-core CPU metrics, such as stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core, remain in the average tier.

The instance exhibits distinct performance tradeoffs. While it demonstrates strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and software compilation speeds, its LLM inference capabilities vary significantly, showing poor text generation speeds for small models and weak prompt processing for large models. Given the high memory-to-vCPU ratio, top-tier database operation benchmarks, and 100 Gbps network baseline, this server is configured for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, caching layers, and network-bound applications rather than heavy single-threaded compute or large-scale LLM inference.
description: The Amazon Web Services r8gn.8xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized server instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. Powered by an AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor, it delivers 32 dedicated physical cores operating at 2.8 GHz and 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB. The instance features a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps and lacks local storage. In benchmark evaluations, the r8gn.8xlarge demonstrates top-tier memory bandwidth, with uncached reads at 414.89 GB/sec and writes at 186.46 GB/sec, alongside top-tier database operations and multi-core Geekbench scores. However, single-core CPU performance and certain LLM inference tasks, such as small-model text generation, show average to poor results. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive databases, high-throughput caching systems, and network-optimized enterprise workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8gn.8xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 256 GB RAM, 100 Gbps network bandwidth, and top-tier memory and database benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8gn.8xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance with 32 Graviton cores, 256 GB RAM, and a 100 Gbps network baseline.
tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated physical cores, high-bandwidth networking, and top-tier memory performance for database and caching workloads.

256.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Top-tier memory bandwidth and database operation benchmark results
Suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and network-bound workloads
